title: ESL change manifest v1
description: Vendorable JSON Schema for the per-change `change.json` manifest defined by ESL v1.0. The `status` and `tier` enums are ESL-OWNED vocabulary (declared here). All other referenced shapes (the SPECTRA spec artifact via `spec_ref`, ECL performatives via the envelope sidecar, CRYSTALIUM layers) are REFERENCED by version elsewhere and MUST NOT be re-declared here. The maker != checker rule cannot be expressed portably in JSON Schema and is enforced by conformance/esl-conformance.sh, not by this schema.

esl_version.description: The ESL version this manifest targets, matching the repo ESL_VERSION stamp (MAJOR.MINOR).

change_id.description: Stable identifier for the change; also the per-change folder name under .spectra/changes/<change_id>/.

maker.description: Eidolon identity that produced the implementation (e.g. vivi, kupo). Non-empty. maker != checker is a conformance-checker rule, not a schema rule.

checker.description: Eidolon identity that verifies the change (e.g. kupo-verifier, vigil). Non-empty and distinct from maker (enforced by the conformance checker).

acceptance_checks.description: Acceptance criteria. An item MAY be EITHER a plain string (a free-form criterion or a bare id that points INTO spec_ref) OR a structured object. The structured object MAY carry the optional EARS form (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax: given/when/then + verify_method). ESL does NOT re-declare SPECTRA's GIVEN/WHEN/THEN format; it carries references INTO it. Backward-compatible: the plain-string and the minimal {id, verify_method} object forms both stay valid.

oneOf.description: Plain-string acceptance criterion (legacy/minimal form). Produces no EARS lint (C7) finding.
Structured acceptance criterion. With given/when/then/verify_method it is the optional EARS form, advisory-linted by C7 in the conformance checker (SHOULD-level; never blocks).

id.description: Acceptance-criterion id defined in the SPECTRA spec at spec_ref (e.g. AC-A1).

given.description: EARS precondition / state. Part of the optional EARS form; advisory-only.

when.description: EARS triggering event. Part of the optional EARS form; advisory-only.

then.description: EARS expected system response (THE SYSTEM SHALL ...). Part of the optional EARS form; advisory-only.

verify_method.description: Reference to how this criterion is verified (e.g. a test path or skill id). A reference, never an inlined verifier.

spec_ref.description: REFERENCE to the SPECTRA-owned spec artifact (relative path to spec.md or spec.yaml), or null for trivial no-spec changes. NEVER an embedded SPECTRA schema.

supersedes.description: OPTIONAL. The prior change_id this change revises (the on-disk bi-temporal diff edge). Mirrors CRYSTALIUM supersedes_id; ESL does not re-declare the memory record.

superseded_by.description: OPTIONAL. A later change_id that supersedes this one.

created_at.description: OPTIONAL. ISO-8601 timestamp the change was proposed.

drift_checked.description: OPTIONAL until archive. MUST be true before status may become `archived` (ESL v1.0 §6; enforced by the conformance checker).

archive_path.description: OPTIONAL. Set on archive to the snapshot location archive/<date>-<change_id>/.

has_code.description: OPTIONAL lifecycle hint declared at propose, read by transition (ESL v1.0 §3.2). true iff the change contains code: a code change MUST pass through the code states (in_progress); a no-code change MAY skip them. Declared once at propose so `transition` need not re-pass it each time; an explicit per-transition flag, if given, overrides this manifest value (back-compat). Not a gate the conformance checker enforces — it is a transition input, not a C1–C6 MUST check.
